questions.subject: Paper-I · General English

options: He told the news to I and my brother.
He told the news to his brother and me.
He told the news to his brother and I.
He told the news to my brother and myself.
questions.solution: He told the news to his brother and me.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A steel rod of 20 mm diameter and 4 m length is subjected to a temperature rise of 40°C. If α = 12×10⁻⁶ /°C and E = 200 GPa, the thermal stress developed when the rod is fully restrained is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 4 m carries a uniformly distributed load of 10 kN/m over its full span. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A full-span UDL on a simply supported beam peaks at mid-span.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=\tfrac{w L^2}{8}$
**Given.** w = 10 kN/m, L = 4 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{10\times4^2}{8}$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=20$ kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The design speed for National Highways as per IRC is:
questions.solution: NH design speed = 100 km/h for plain, 80 km/h for rolling terrain as per IRC.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Hoyer's system is used for:
questions.solution: Hoyer = long-line pre-tensioning system using wire strands.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The property of a material which allows it to be drawn into wires is called:
questions.solution: Ductility = ability to be drawn into wires; malleability = ability to be hammered into sheets.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A cantilever of span 5 m carries a uniformly distributed load of 5 kN/m over its entire length. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A full-span UDL on a cantilever produces its maximum moment at the fixed support.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=\tfrac{w L^2}{2}$
**Given.** w = 5 kN/m, L = 5 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{5\times5^2}{2}$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=62.5$ kNm at the fixed end.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Mechanical extra widening on a horizontal curve is provided predominantly on the:
questions.solution: Off-tracking sweeps the rear wheels toward the inside of the curve, so the mechanical component of extra widening is given on the inner side.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Prestressed concrete is most commonly used in:
options: Bridges, water tanks, and long-span structures
questions.solution: PSC ideal for bridges, tanks, poles, sleepers, long-span structures.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The ratio of maximum to average shear stress for a solid circular cross-section is:
questions.solution: For a circle the factor is 4/3 (the 3/2 value belongs to a rectangle) — a common trap.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A propped cantilever (one end fixed, one simple) has a degree of static indeterminacy of:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The longest National Highway in India is:
questions.solution: NH 44 = longest (~3745 km), connecting Srinagar to Kanyakumari.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In prestressed concrete, high tensile steel is used because:
options: It has higher modulus of elasticity
Its high strength compensates for losses in prestress
questions.solution: HT steel (f_pk up to 1860 MPa) used to leave sufficient prestress after losses.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 6 m carries a uniformly distributed load of 20 kN/m over its full span. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A full-span UDL on a simply supported beam peaks at mid-span.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=\tfrac{w L^2}{8}$
**Given.** w = 20 kN/m, L = 6 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{20\times6^2}{8}$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=90$ kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The superelevation for a design speed of 100 km/h and radius 150 m (e = V²/127R) is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The loss of prestress due to elastic shortening of concrete is:
options: m × f_c where m = modular ratio, f_c = stress in concrete at steel level
questions.solution: Loss = m×f_c (where m = E_s/E_c, f_c = stress in concrete at CG of steel).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The section modulus of a rectangular section 150 mm wide and 450 mm deep is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The stiffness of a prismatic beam with far end fixed (k = M/θ) is:
questions.solution: Stiffness = 4EI/L for prismatic member with far end fixed.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A railway BG track has a sleeper density of M+7. If the rail length is 13 m, the number of sleepers per rail length is:
questions.solution: M = rail length in m = 13. M+7 = 20 sleepers per rail length for BG.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a simply supported prestressed beam with concentric tendon, the stress at the bottom fibre at mid-span under self-weight is:
questions.solution: Bottom stress = P/A - M/Z (prestress subtracts from tensile stress).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 3 m carries a point load of 25 kN at 2.5 m from the left support. The reaction at the left support is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a perfect plane truss, the relation between members m and joints j is:
questions.solution: A statically determinate (perfect) plane truss satisfies m = 2j − 3.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The superelevation for a design speed of 60 km/h and radius 300 m (e = V²/127R) is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a PSC slab, using draped tendons provides:
options: Varying eccentricity to match the bending moment diagram
questions.solution: Draped = parabolic profile with max eccentricity where BM is max.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: At a point σx = 120 MPa, σy = 60 MPa and τxy = 20 MPa. The major principal stress is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The major principal stress is the centre of Mohr's circle plus its radius.
**Formula.** $\sigma_1=\tfrac{\sigma_x+\sigma_y}{2}+\sqrt{\left(\tfrac{\sigma_x-\sigma_y}{2}\right)^2+\tau_{xy}^2}$
**Given.** σx = 120 MPa, σy = 60 MPa, τxy = 20 MPa.
**Working.** Centre $=90$, radius $=36.06$; $\sigma_1=90+36.06$.
**Answer.** $\sigma_1=126.06$ MPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 8 m carries a central point load of 40 kN. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A central point load on a simply supported beam gives maximum bending moment at mid-span.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=\tfrac{W L}{4}$
**Given.** W = 40 kN, L = 8 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{40\times8}{4}$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=80$ kNm at mid-span.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The test for ductility of bitumen is conducted at:
questions.solution: Ductility test at 25°C with 5 cm/min pull rate.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The FIP/CEB recommendations classify prestress losses as:
options: Immediate (elastic, friction, anchorage) and time-dependent (creep, shrinkage, relaxation)
questions.solution: Immediate: elastic shortening, friction, anchorage slip. Time: creep, shrinkage, relaxation.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The principal stresses at a point are 70 MPa and 30 MPa. The normal stress on the plane carrying the maximum shear stress is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** On the plane of maximum shear the normal stress equals the centre of Mohr's circle.
**Formula.** $\sigma_n=\tfrac{\sigma_1+\sigma_3}{2}$
**Given.** σ₁ = 70 MPa, σ₃ = 30 MPa.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{70+30}{2}$.
**Answer.** $\sigma_n=50$ MPa.

figure.caption: Centre of Mohr's circle = mean normal stress

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The maximum water-cement ratio for M25 concrete as per IS 456 for moderate exposure is:
questions.solution: Max w/c for M25, moderate exposure = 0.50 (IS 456 Table 5).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A pretensioned beam has concentric tendon. The stress distribution at mid-span under self-weight is:
questions.solution: Concentric prestress + BM → combined uniform + linear stress.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the IS 456 limit-state stress block, the total compressive force carried by the concrete equals:
questions.solution: The area of the parabolic-rectangular stress block gives C = 0.36 fck b xu; 0.42 xu is the depth of its centroid, a value candidates often confuse with the force coefficient.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Freyssinet system of prestressing uses:
questions.solution: Freyssinet = multi-wire strands anchored in conical holes with wedges.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The instrument used to measure horizontal and vertical angles is the:
questions.solution: A theodolite measures both horizontal and vertical angles.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A fixed beam of span 3 m carries a central point load of 30 kN. The fixed-end moment at each support is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Bernoulli's equation is applicable for:
options: Steady, incompressible, inviscid flow along a streamline
questions.solution: Bernoulli: steady, incompressible, inviscid, along streamline.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The differential settlement of a foundation can be reduced by:
options: Providing a stiff raft foundation or increasing footing size
questions.solution: Raft foundation = rigid to distribute load and reduce differential settlement.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A cantilever of span 3 m carries a UDL of 15 kN/m over its full length. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution: BM_max = wL²/2 = 15×3²/2 = 67.5 kNm at fixed end.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The permissible limit of chloride in drinking water as per IS 10500 is:
questions.solution: Permissible Cl⁻ = 250 mg/L, max (no alternative source) = 1000 mg/L.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The group index of a soil for pavement design depends on:
options: LL, PL, and % passing 75 µm sieve
questions.solution: GI = 0.2a + 0.005ac + 0.01bd where a,b,c,d from LL, PL, and fines %.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: If the fore bearing of a line is 45°, its back bearing is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the IS 456 limit state stress block, the line of action of the compressive force lies from the extreme compression fibre at:
questions.solution: The centroid of the IS 456 stress block is at 0.42xu from the extreme fibre.

questions.solution: Skid friction f = 0.35-0.40 for design speed.

questions.solution: Fe415 denotes a yield strength of 415 MPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a singly reinforced section (Fe415, M20 concrete, width 300 mm, effective depth 550 mm), the limiting moment of resistance (Mu,lim = 0.138 fck b d²) is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** For a balanced (limiting) Fe415 section the moment of resistance is fixed by the coefficient 0.138 in $f_{ck}\,b\,d^2$.
**Formula.** $M_{u,lim}=0.138\,f_{ck}\,b\,d^2$
**Given.** f_ck = 20 MPa, b = 300 mm, d = 550 mm.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{0.138\times20\times300\times550^2}{10^6}$ kNm.
**Answer.** $M_{u,lim}\approx 250.5$ kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In plane table surveying, the method of intersection is used for:
options: Locating points by drawing rays from two stations
questions.solution: Intersection: point fixed by intersection of rays from two known stations.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Reynold's transport theorem is used to:
options: Convert system laws to control volume formulation
questions.solution: RTT = link between system (Lagrangian) and CV (Eulerian) approaches.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: RL of a benchmark is 120 m. With back sight 1.5 m and fore sight 2.2 m, the RL of the next point is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The discharge over a rectangular weir (Cd = 0.62) of length 3 m under a head of 0.5 m is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Discharge over a sharp-crested rectangular weir varies with the 3/2 power of the head.
**Formula.** $Q=\tfrac23 C_d\sqrt{2g}\,L\,H^{3/2}$
**Given.** C_d = 0.62, L = 3 m, H = 0.5 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac23\times0.62\times\sqrt{2\times9.81}\times3\times0.5^{1.5}$.
**Answer.** $Q\approx 1.94$ m³/s.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The sensitivity of a bubble tube depends on:
options: Radius of curvature of the tube
questions.solution: Sensitivity = 1 radian/R = 206265/R seconds. Larger R = more sensitive.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: When a fluid flows over a flat plate, the flow transitions from laminar to turbulent at Re ~:
questions.solution: Transition Re for flat plate boundary layer ~ 5×10⁵.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A total station essentially combines a theodolite with:
questions.solution: A total station integrates an electronic theodolite with an EDM and data recorder.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 4 m carries a central point load of 30 kN. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A central point load on a simply supported beam gives maximum bending moment at mid-span.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=\tfrac{W L}{4}$
**Given.** W = 30 kN, L = 4 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{30\times4}{4}$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=30$ kNm at mid-span.

questions.solution: Stiffness method: displacements are primary unknowns (d = K⁻¹F).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The centre of pressure for a vertical plane surface is:
questions.solution: h_cp = h_c + I/(Ah_c) → always below centroid for vertical surfaces.

questions.solution: σ = My/I, max y = extreme fibre.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: At a point σx = 80 MPa, σy = 40 MPa and τxy = 40 MPa. The major principal stress is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The major principal stress is the centre of Mohr's circle plus its radius.
**Formula.** $\sigma_1=\tfrac{\sigma_x+\sigma_y}{2}+\sqrt{\left(\tfrac{\sigma_x-\sigma_y}{2}\right)^2+\tau_{xy}^2}$
**Given.** σx = 80 MPa, σy = 40 MPa, τxy = 40 MPa.
**Working.** Centre $=60$, radius $=44.72$; $\sigma_1=60+44.72$.
**Answer.** $\sigma_1=104.72$ MPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: At a point the major and minor principal stresses are 60 MPa and 20 MPa. From Mohr's circle, the maximum in-plane shear stress is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The maximum in-plane shear stress equals the radius of Mohr's circle.
**Formula.** $\tau_{max}=\tfrac{\sigma_1-\sigma_3}{2}$
**Given.** σ₁ = 60 MPa, σ₃ = 20 MPa.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{60-20}{2}$.
**Answer.** $\tau_{max}=20$ MPa.

questions.stem: A cantilever of span 4 m carries a UDL of 8 kN/m over its full length. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution: BM_max = wL²/2 = 8×4²/2 = 64 kNm at fixed end.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Euler's critical buckling load of a column is proportional to:
questions.solution: Pcr = π²EI/Le², so it varies inversely with the square of effective length.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: At a point σx = 120 MPa, σy = 60 MPa and τxy = 30 MPa. The major principal stress is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The major principal stress is the centre of Mohr's circle plus its radius.
**Formula.** $\sigma_1=\tfrac{\sigma_x+\sigma_y}{2}+\sqrt{\left(\tfrac{\sigma_x-\sigma_y}{2}\right)^2+\tau_{xy}^2}$
**Given.** σx = 120 MPa, σy = 60 MPa, τxy = 30 MPa.
**Working.** Centre $=90$, radius $=42.43$; $\sigma_1=90+42.43$.
**Answer.** $\sigma_1=132.43$ MPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A pin-ended column (E = 200 GPa, I = 16×10⁻⁶ m⁴, length 3 m) has an Euler buckling load of about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A pin-ended column buckles at the Euler critical load with effective length equal to the actual length.
**Formula.** $P_{cr}=\tfrac{\pi^2 E I}{L^2}$
**Given.** E = 200 GPa, I = 16×10⁻⁶ m⁴, L = 3 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{\pi^2\times200\times10^9\times16\times10^{-6}}{3^2}$ N.
**Answer.** $P_{cr}\approx 3509.2$ kN.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: At a point σx = 80 MPa, σy = 60 MPa and τxy = 20 MPa. The major principal stress is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The major principal stress is the centre of Mohr's circle plus its radius.
**Formula.** $\sigma_1=\tfrac{\sigma_x+\sigma_y}{2}+\sqrt{\left(\tfrac{\sigma_x-\sigma_y}{2}\right)^2+\tau_{xy}^2}$
**Given.** σx = 80 MPa, σy = 60 MPa, τxy = 20 MPa.
**Working.** Centre $=70$, radius $=22.36$; $\sigma_1=70+22.36$.
**Answer.** $\sigma_1=92.36$ MPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge

questions.solution: f = φ(Re, ε/D) in turbulent regime (Moody chart).

questions.solution: Shrinkage loss = ε_sh × E_s (where ε_sh ~ 0.0003 for pre-tensioning).

questions.solution: Total = p/γ + z + v²/2g. Piezometric = p/γ + z.
